biases_detected: shell_tool_preference_violations:RECURRING:STABLE — unit-18 (ls -la via Bash instead of Glob, find instead of Glob in compound search); unit-431b5ec9-1 (ls -dt via Bash x2, python3 inline JSON x4+ for transcript/task parsing, find compound across multiple calls); unit-431b5ec9-4 (python3 inline JSON x3 for task dir inspection, ls -t via Bash); unit-431b5ec9-5 (ls -t via Bash, find via Bash, python3 inline JSON x3, grep -n via Bash directly instead of Grep tool); 4 of 7 tool-using units; thirty-ninth+ consecutive cycle above 50%, zero downward trajectory

bash_retry_without_diagnosis:RECURRING:STABLE — unit-431b5ec9-1 (15+ consecutive Bash investigation calls across task/todo/session-note system with incremental pattern variation before AskUserQuestion pivot; iteration count meets or exceeds prior cycle peak of 13+; no diagnostic pivot between attempts; structurally identical to prior three cycles; no improvement trajectory)

compound_command_overreach:RECURRING:WORSENING — unit-431b5ec9-1 (python3 heredoc + ls + rg + find compounds across multiple calls; one Bash call fails success=false); unit-431b5ec9-4 (python3 inline + ls + rg multi-pipe compound; first Bash call fails success=false triggering re-attempt); unit-431b5ec9-5 (python3 heredoc + rg + stat + find compounds x3; two Bash calls fail success=false); 3 units vs prior cycle 2-unit plateau — escalated from STABLE

optimistic_edit_without_read:RECURRING:STABLE — unit-431b5ec9-4 (Edit runtime-notes.md fails success=false; Edit model.md requires 3 consecutive calls to clean rendering artifacts from freshly written file; re-edited without re-reading modified state; plateau maintained from prior cycles at similar failure rate)

mcp_retry_without_diagnosis:RECURRING:POSSIBLY_IMPROVING — unit-6412e83a-5 (mcp__file-tools__list_sheets called x2 both fail, then pivot to Bash fallback; only 2 sequential failures before fallback vs prior cycle x3 on mcp__render__list_logs; tool identity differs so cross-tool comparison is weak, but count reduction is notable; insufficient to confirm trend from one data point)

direct_grep_via_bash:NEW — unit-431b5ec9-5 (bare grep -n call in Bash for runtime-notes.md line search; rules/shell.md mandates rg or Grep tool, and a PreToolUse hook reportedly blocks grep; distinct from the rg+grep pipe-chain sub-variant flagged under shell_tool_preference_violations in prior cycles — this is a standalone grep invocation, suggesting either hook miss or hook non-coverage of this call shape)

hook_false_positive_content_match:ABSENT_THIS_CYCLE — not observed in any of the 7 units; fourth prior consecutive cycle established as structural fixture; single-cycle absence insufficient to downgrade trajectory but worth monitoring
recommendations: direct_grep_via_bash is a new variant warranting its own atone entry distinct from the broader shell_tool_preference cluster; verify whether the grep-block PreToolUse hook covers this exact call shape or whether a coverage gap exists — if hook fired and was bypassed, that is S3

compound_command_overreach escalated from STABLE to WORSENING (3 units, 2 Bash failures from overreach); two consecutive cycles of same-or-higher unit count with success=false outcomes suggest the pattern is not self-correcting — hook-level enforcement blocking heredoc+pipe compound Bash calls above a complexity threshold is the next lever

shell_tool_preference_violations and bash_retry_without_diagnosis have had zero improvement across 39+ cycles under advisory-only recommendations; only mechanical enforcement at the hook layer has any remaining probability of impact; further text-level recommendations are unlikely to change the trajectory
mcp_retry_without_diagnosis shows one data point of possible improvement; monitor next 2-3 cycles before updating trajectory; do not recommend fixes until trend confirms

optimistic_edit_without_read plateau holds; the trigger is consistently Write-then-verify flows where written content contains rendering artifacts; the post-Write render-check rule in rules/sub-agent-outputs.md is documented but not being applied — a Stop-hook render-check on recently written .md files would catch this mechanically
